explanation: Net North movement = 10 - 6 = 4 km. East movement = 3 km. Shortest distance = √(4² + 3²) = 5 km. Direction is North-East.

question: Anil left home and cycled 10 km Southwards, turned right and cycled 5 km & turned right and cycled 10 km & turned left and cycled 10 km. How many kilometers will he have to cycle to reach his home straight?
explanation: The vertical moves cancel out (10 km South then 10 km North). The horizontal moves are 5 km West and then another 10 km West. Total straight line distance back home = 5 + 10 = 15 km.

question: If a person is standing on his head facing South, to which direction will his right hand point?
explanation: When standing normally facing South, the right hand points West. When inverted (standing on head) facing South, the hands reverse orientation, so the right hand points East.

question: Y is to the East of X, which is to the North of Z. If P is to the South of Z, then in which direction of Y is P?
explanation: Z is South of X. Y is East of X. P is further South of Z. Plotting this on a grid shows that P is located to the South-West of Y.

question: A clock shows 4:30. If the minute hand points towards East, in which direction will the hour hand point?
explanation: At 4:30, the minute hand normally points South (6 o'clock position). If South is labeled East (90 deg ACW shift), the hour hand which is normally between South and East (at 4:30 position) shifts to North-East.

question: Two cars start from the same place at the same time. One goes North at 40 km/hr and the other goes West at 30 km/hr. What will be the distance between them after 2 hours?
explanation: Distance covered North = 40 * 2 = 80 km. Distance covered West = 30 * 2 = 60 km. Distance apart = √(80² + 60²) = √10000 = 100 km.

question: A man walks 1 km towards East and then he turns to South and walks 5 km. Again he turns to East and walks 2 km, after this he turns to North and walks 9 km. Now, how far is he from his starting point?
explanation: Total East distance = 1 + 2 = 3 km. Net North distance = 9 - 5 = 4 km. Total distance = √(3² + 4²) = 5 km.

question: One evening before sunset Rekha and Hema were talking to each other face to face. If Hema's shadow was exactly to the right of Hema, which direction was Rekha facing?
explanation: In the evening shadows fall East. Since Hema's shadow falls to her right, Hema must be facing North. Rekha is facing Hema face-to-face, so Rekha is facing South.

question: A rat runs 20' towards East and turns to right, runs 10' and turns to right, runs 9' and again turns to left, runs 5' and then turns to left, runs 12' and finally turns to left and runs 6'. Now, which direction is the rat facing?

question: A man is facing West. He turns 45 degrees in the clockwise direction and then another 180 degrees in the same direction and then 270 degrees in the anti-clockwise direction. Find which direction he is facing now.
explanation: Net shift = +45 + 180 - 270 = -45 degrees (ACW). 45 degrees anti-clockwise from West is South-West.

question: Starting from a point P, Sachin walked 20 meters towards South. He turned left and walked 30 meters. He then turned left and walked 20 meters. He again turned left and walked 40 meters and reached a point Q. How far and in which direction is the point Q from the point P?
explanation: Vertical moves cancel out (20m South, 20m North). Horizontal moves: 30m East, then 40m West. Net position is 10 meters West of P.

question: Deepak starts walking straight towards East. After walking 75 meters, he turns to the left and walks 25 meters straight. Again he turns to the left and walks a distance of 40 meters straight. Again he turns to the left and walks a distance of 25 meters. How far is he from the starting point?
explanation: Vertical moves cancel out (25m North, 25m South). Horizontal moves: 75m East, then 40m West. Net distance = 75 - 40 = 35 meters.

question: Sanjeev walks 10 meters North, turns right and walks 10 meters, turns right and walks 10 meters, then turns left and walks 5 meters. How far is he from his initial position?
explanation: The North and South movements of 10m cancel out. Horizontal movements are 10m East and 5m East. Total distance = 10 + 5 = 15 meters.

question: If North-West becomes South, South-West becomes East and so on, what will North become?
explanation: The mapping rotates directions by 135 degrees clockwise. Shifting North by 135 degrees clockwise results in South-East.

question: LaxMAN went 15 kms to the West from his house, then turned left and walked 20 kms. He then turned East and walked 25 kms and finally turning left covered 20 kms. How far was he from his house?
explanation: Vertical segments cancel (20 km South, 20 km North). Horizontal shifts: 15 km West, then 25 km East. Net distance = 25 - 15 = 10 km.

question: A boy rides his bicycle Northwards, then turns left and rides 1 km and then turns left and rides 2 km. He finds himself exactly 1 km West of his starting point. How far did he ride Northwards initially?
explanation: He turned left (West) and rode 1 km, then turned left (South) and rode 2 km. To end up exactly West of his starting point, his Southward run must equal his initial Northward run. So, initial run = 2 km.

question: Devansh starts from his house and walks 2 km North, then turns right and walks 1 km, then turns right again and walks 2 km. In which direction is he going now?
